context: Building blocks a coding project downloads for itself. Often the biggest folder in a project — the project can fetch them all again.
restoreNote: Open the project and run its install command (pnpm install or npm install) — everything comes back.

context: A private workspace a Python project sets up for itself. The project can rebuild it from scratch.
restoreNote: The project recreates it with one command (uv sync or python -m venv).

context: Leftover build files from a Rust coding project — usually. Any folder named 'target' matches this rule, so take a quick look inside before deleting.
restoreNote: The project rebuilds it with one command (cargo build).

context: Temporary files a Next.js website project makes while building. Recreated every time the site is built.
restoreNote: The project rebuilds it with one command (next build).

context: Test web browsers downloaded by a developer tool called Playwright. Only needed while testing websites.
restoreNote: The tool downloads them again when needed (npx playwright install).

context: A half-downloaded app update waiting to be installed. The app simply downloads its next update again.
restoreNote: Nothing to do — the app fetches its next update by itself.

context: Support files a developer tool downloaded once and can download again.
restoreNote: Nothing to do — re-downloaded automatically when needed.

context: Temporary files kept by the OpenAI Codex assistant. It rebuilds them next time it runs.
restoreNote: Nothing to do — recreated automatically.

context: Downloads kept by CocoaPods, a tool used when building iPhone/Mac apps. It fetches them again when needed.
restoreNote: Nothing to do — re-downloaded on the next build.

context: Old installer downloads kept by Homebrew (a Mac app installer). Safe to clear — it downloads fresh copies when needed.
restoreNote: Nothing to do — Homebrew re-downloads anything it needs.

context: An app's scratch space for temporary files. Apps rebuild this on their own; a running app may briefly miss it.
restoreNote: Nothing to do — the app rebuilds it next time it opens.

context: Temporary files Xcode (Apple's app-building tool) creates while building apps. It remakes them automatically.
restoreNote: Nothing to do — Xcode rebuilds this on the next build (first build is slower).

context: Pretend iPhones and Apple Watches used for testing apps on a Mac. Xcode can set them up again.
restoreNote: Xcode recreates simulators when you next need one.

context: Working files kept by a code-checking tool. Rebuilt automatically next time it runs.
restoreNote: Nothing to do — recreated on next use.

context: Downloaded AI models from Hugging Face. Can be very large — and can be downloaded again anytime.
restoreNote: Downloads again automatically next time a tool asks for the model (can take a while for big models).

context: A test web browser downloaded by a developer tool called Puppeteer.
restoreNote: The tool downloads it again when needed.

context: Support files downloaded by the OpenAI Codex assistant. Fetched again automatically when needed.
restoreNote: Nothing to do — re-downloaded on next use.

context: Downloads kept by uv, a Python tool, so future installs are faster. Safe to clear.
restoreNote: Nothing to do — refilled on the next install.

context: A tool's scratch space for temporary files. Tools rebuild this on their own.
restoreNote: Nothing to do — rebuilt next time the tool runs.

context: Downloads kept by npm, a coding tool, so future installs are faster. Safe to clear.
restoreNote: Nothing to do — refilled on the next install.

context: Downloads kept by pnpm, a coding tool. Deleting means future installs re-download instead of reusing.
restoreNote: Nothing to do — refilled on the next install.

context: Downloaded AI models used by Ollama. These are huge files that can be downloaded again anytime.
restoreNote: Get a model back with: ollama pull <model name> (big download).

context: AI models and add-ons downloaded by the LM Studio app. Can be downloaded again inside the app.
restoreNote: Open LM Studio and download the model again.

context: Something in your Downloads folder you haven't touched in months — often an installer or zip you already used. Could still be something you made, so check first.
restoreNote: If it came from the internet you can usually download it again — but look before you delete.

context: An app installer (.dmg). If the app is already installed, the installer is no longer needed.
restoreNote: Download it again from the app's website if you ever need it.

context: Downloads kept by a Rust coding tool so future builds are faster. Safe to clear.
restoreNote: Nothing to do — re-downloaded on the next build.

context: Downloads and temporary files kept by Gradle, an app-building tool. Safe to clear.
restoreNote: Nothing to do — re-downloaded on the next build.

context: Docker Desktop's entire storage — every container, image, and saved volume lives in here. Deleting wipes all of it, including data you may not be able to get back.
restoreNote: Images can be downloaded or rebuilt, but saved volumes are gone for good — make sure nothing important is inside first.

context: Support files Xcode copied from iPhones that were plugged in before. Copied again automatically next time the device connects.
restoreNote: Nothing to do — recreated when the device is next plugged in.
